query: I want to write a PreToolUse hook that intercepts every Bash invocation and blocks commands that match a deny list (rm -rf /, git push --force on main, dangerously-skip-permissions). Walk me through writing and registering it.
expected_behavior: Describes a hook script that reads the tool call payload (JSON on stdin) and emits a structured response (JSON on stdout with allow/block plus reason), and explains the exit-code or stdout contract used to gate the call
Recommends registering the hook in settings.json (or a plugin manifest) with event=PreToolUse and a matcher that scopes it to the Bash tool — not unscoped on every tool
Calls out that the hook itself runs with the user's privileges and must be source-reviewed, audit-logged, and pinned just like any elevated-permission artifact
